notes_for_minecraft.shape: Bowl/vase shape - circular footprint that widens from base to top. Base ~40m diameter, top ~60m diameter, 39.5m tall. In Minecraft at 1:1 scale that is roughly 40 blocks wide at base, 60 at top, 40 blocks tall.
notes_for_minecraft.facade: Fully mirrored reflective glass panels. In Minecraft consider using light_blue_stained_glass or ice blocks for the reflective effect.
notes_for_minecraft.rooftop: Rooftop forest garden with 75 multi-stemmed birch trees (Betula pubescens), fir trees, grasses, and a restaurant. Birch trees grow to max 10m tall.
notes_for_minecraft.atrium: Central atrium is 40m high, 28m wide, 6m deep. Five zigzagging staircases (Piranesi-style) cross the space, plus two glass elevator shafts and one large art elevator. Artist Marieke van Diemen's floating display cases fill the atrium.
notes_for_minecraft.floor_program: Ground floor: entrance foyer. Floor 1: atrium floor. Floor 2: education centre. Floors 3-4: galleries. Floor 5: suite. Floors 1-5: storage depots around the perimeter.

architectural_drawings.description: Cross-section showing bowl shape, atrium with zigzag staircases, floor levels, and rooftop garden
architectural_drawings.notes: ArchDaily gallery image 36 - likely a section drawing. Browse the full gallery at archdaily.com/948370 (images 36-43 typically contain drawings).

architectural_drawings.description: Architectural drawings and sections from MVRDV
architectural_drawings.notes: MVRDV official project page contains section drawings, floor plans, and elevations. Scroll through the full image gallery.

architectural_drawings.description: Design evolution in 9 steps - includes early sketches and diagrams
architectural_drawings.notes: Official museum page showing how the bowl shape was developed. Includes the famous IKEA Blanda Blank bowl that inspired the form.

architectural_drawings.description: Archilovers project page with technical drawings
architectural_drawings.notes: Archilovers typically includes floor plans and sections submitted by the architect.
Spanish architecture journal - typically publishes floor plans and sections.
Detail magazine specializes in construction details - likely has facade sections and structural details.

best_pages_to_browse_manually.why: 43-image gallery with photos AND architectural drawings (sections, plans, elevations). The most complete single source.
Official MVRDV page with high-res photos and drawings from the architect.
Shows the design process and shape development - great for understanding the geometry.
58 freely licensed images - various angles and construction stages.
Excellent interior photos showing atrium, staircases, and storage areas.
